proposals.title: CS/CS/HJR 203
proposals.text: CS/CS/HJR 203 Elimination of Non-school Property for Homesteads dead Central House-passed proposal in the 2026 property tax elimination debate.

proposals.title: Related 2026 House proposals
proposals.text: Related 2026 House proposals Related homestead, non-school ad valorem, and assessment-cap proposals listed with HJR 203 historical_context_only Relevant as comparison proposals, but not active vehicles for the current status summary.

timeline.title: HJR 203 was filed
timeline.text: 2025-10-16 HJR 203 was filed The House property-tax proposal that became CS/CS/HJR 203 entered the 2026 regular-session bill record. This created the primary House vehicle tracked in the 2026 elimination debate.

timeline.title: CS/CS/HJR 203 passed the House
timeline.text: 2026-02-19 CS/CS/HJR 203 passed the House The House passed CS/CS/HJR 203 by an 80-30 vote. The proposal cleared one chamber but still needed Senate approval to reach the ballot.

timeline.title: CS/CS/HJR 203 was referred to Senate Appropriations
timeline.text: 2026-02-19 CS/CS/HJR 203 was referred to Senate Appropriations After House passage, the Senate received the proposal and referred it to Appropriations. Senate committee movement became the remaining regular-session path for that proposal.

timeline.title: CS/CS/HJR 203 died in Senate Appropriations
timeline.text: 2026-03-13 CS/CS/HJR 203 died in Senate Appropriations The House-passed property-tax proposal did not advance through the Senate before the end of the regular session. This ended the regular-session path for CS/CS/HJR 203.

timeline.title: Senate memo described Special Session D legislation
timeline.text: 2026-04-24 Senate memo described Special Session D legislation The Senate memo identified expected Special Session D legislation on redistricting, artificial intelligence, public records, and medical freedom. The official memo did not describe a property-tax elimination vehicle among the listed Special Session D items.

timeline.title: Special Session D bill-actions list showed no tracked property-tax elimination proposal
timeline.text: 2026-04-28 Special Session D bill-actions list showed no tracked property-tax elimination proposal The April 28 Senate bill-actions list reviewed for this tracker showed Special Session D activity on redistricting, AI, public records, medical freedom, and state reapportionment items. This supports the current status label that no tracked property-tax elimination proposal was active on that reviewed list.

overview.title: HJR 203 entered the House record
overview.text: 2025-10-16 Filed HJR 203 entered the House record The House property-tax proposal that became CS/CS/HJR 203 was filed for the 2026 regular session. This created the primary House vehicle tracked in the elimination debate. completed

overview.title: House approval completed
overview.text: 2026-02-19 House House approval completed CS/CS/HJR 203 passed the Florida House by an 80-30 vote. A legislative constitutional amendment also needed Senate approval before any ballot step. completed

overview.title: Senate path blocked
overview.text: 2026-03-13 Senate Senate path blocked CS/CS/HJR 203 died in Senate Appropriations. This ended the regular-session path for that specific House proposal. blocked

overview.title: No Special Session D vehicle identified
overview.text: 2026-04-28 Vehicle No Special Session D vehicle identified No tracked property-tax elimination proposal appeared on the April 28, 2026 Special Session D bill-actions list reviewed for this tracker. A new special session call, HJR, or SJR would be needed to reopen a legislative route. watching

overview.title: Ballot listing not reached
overview.text: Future checkpoint Ballot Ballot listing not reached A legislative constitutional amendment would still need voter approval before it could change the Florida Constitution. The Department of State ballot step comes after a measure advances through the required constitutional-amendment path. not_started
